site.office: King County District Court Judge · Position 1, Northeast Division
site.metaDescription: Bianca Tse is running for King County District Court Judge, Position 1, Northeast Division. Nearly 30 years as a Washington attorney, former King County prosecutor, and current pro tem judge — ready on day one.

hero.headline: Ready on Day One
hero.subhead: Bianca Tse is the only former King County prosecutor in this race — a Bellevue attorney with nearly 30 years in Washington courtrooms, ready to serve as a fair and thoughtful judge.

about.headline: Nearly 30 years in Washington courtrooms
about.body:
Bianca Tse has practiced law in Washington for nearly 30 years. As the only former King County Deputy Prosecutor in this race, she understands how decisions affect the safety, fairness and well-being of the community.  These are the skills that make an effective and fair judge.
Bianca is the only candidate currently serving as a Judge Pro Tem for King County District Court and other cities on the Eastside.  She is already doing the work of the position she seeks. For the residents of the community, electing a judge with current pro tem experience means choosing someone who understands the responsibilities of the bench and has a proven record of administering justice. A judge's decisions affect individuals, families, businesses, and public confidence in the legal system. Experience presiding over cases helps ensure that the court operates efficiently, that litigants receive a fair hearing, and that decisions are made thoughtfully, impartially, and in accordance with the law.
Current pro tem judicial service also demonstrates an ongoing commitment to public service and the administration of justice. It reflects the confidence that the legal community has placed in the candidate to serve in a judicial capacity and provides practical experience that supports a smooth transition to a full-time judicial role. Voters benefit from electing a judge who has already demonstrated the ability to lead a courtroom with fairness, professionalism, and impartiality.
Bianca began her legal career in public service at the King County Prosecutor's office and tried dozens of cases in front of juries.  As a skilled prosecutor she helped to hold offenders accountable while ensuring that constitutional rights are protected and that decisions are based on the evidence and the interests of justice. Her work contributed to safer neighborhoods, supported victims during difficult circumstances, and helped maintain public confidence in the criminal justice system.
Today she's the Principal of her own firm, serving individuals, families, and small businesses across the Eastside. As a civil attorney, she's guided hundreds of clients in resolving disputes including multi-million dollar real estate and business transactions.
She earned her law degree from Tulane University and her undergraduate degree from the University of Washington. Her career comes down to a few things she believes: respect the law, treat people fairly, and make sure the justice system works for everyone, not just some.
She gives her time to the Eastside, too. She's a former board member and current development committee member of a local nonprofit that helps women and families in need. She's led a Girl Scout troop and volunteered for leadership roles at Bellevue schools.
about.photoCaption: Bianca with community members on the Eastside.

issues.headline: The experience this court needs
issues.intro: Every case matters. Everyone who comes through the courthouse doors deserves to be treated with respect and dignity — no matter why they are there.

items.title: Proven in Court
items.description: Nearly 30 years of legal practice in Washington. A former Deputy Prosecuting Attorney with both felony and misdemeanor trial experience, and a full command of evidence and trial procedure.

items.title: Community Roots
items.description: A trusted Bellevue attorney who has represented individuals, families, and small businesses across the Eastside, and gives her time to local schools and nonprofits.

items.title: Ready on Day One
items.description: Already serving as a pro tem judge at King County District Court and other Eastside municipal courts — prepared to step in and do the work from the first day on the bench.

endorsements.subtitle: Trusted by judges, elected leaders, and community organizations
